items.title: Lists and stable keys: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Array index is always the best key because it is unique during one render.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use stable data identity as key when items can reorder, insert, or delete; positional indexes can attach state to the wrong item.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Use stable data identity as key when items can reorder, insert, or delete; positional indexes can attach state to the wrong item.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.title: Lists and stable keys: verified implementation facet
